problem_addressed: Deterministic embeddings from standard Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle to capture uncertainties arising from ambiguities in visual/textual descriptions and multiple possible correspondences between images and texts.
core_claim: GroVE achieves state-of-the-art uncertainty calibration by generating probabilistic embeddings from frozen VLMs through a post-hoc Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model approach.
key_contributions: GroVE framework for post-hoc probabilistic embeddings from frozen VLMs
GPLVM-based shared latent space learning with single-modal reconstruction and cross-modal alignment
Superior uncertainty calibration across cross-modal retrieval, VQA, and active learning tasks
novelty: Unlike existing methods requiring probabilistic embeddings during VLM training (demanding large datasets), GroVE leverages frozen VLMs like CLIP without retraining, using GPLVM to learn uncertainty-aware representations post-hoc.
method_summary: GroVE builds a Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model (GPLVM) to learn a shared low-dimensional latent space where image and text inputs map to unified representations, optimized through single-modal embedding reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives.
method_components: Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model (GPLVM) for latent space learning
key_innovation: The post-hoc application of GPLVM to frozen VLMs to generate uncertainty-aware probabilistic embeddings without requiring retraining or large datasets, enabling uncertainty quantification for powerful pre-trained models like CLIP.
assumptions: Frozen VLMs provide sufficient initial representations for latent space learning
Shared low-dimensional latent space can capture cross-modal relationships
Uncertainty can be effectively modeled through Gaussian Processes
theoretical_grounding: The predictive distribution is formulated as p(g*) = ∫ p(g*|x*)p(x*|z*, zM)dx*, where uncertainty in x* increases variance in the predictive distribution through Σ*, accounting for both latent space uncertainty and inherent prediction noise.
headline_result: 0.79±0.02 KL-Divergence score for Image-to-Text alignment on COCO
improvement_over_baseline: State-of-the-art performance in uncertainty calibration for cross-modal retrieval, active learning and VQA
best_result: 0.79±0.02 KL-Divergence score for Image-to-Text alignment on COCO
title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Frozen Vision-Language Models: Uncertainty Quantification with Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models
